attacking_style: Opportunistic late attacks on technical finishes (700m observed), targeting transitional stages where pure sprinter teams struggle with positioning. Also employs intermediate sprint ambushes to disrupt GC calculations.
team_tactics: Ineos uses him as versatile utility rider: valley/early climb tempo control in mountains despite non-climber profile, positioned for bonus denial rather than stage wins in Grand Tours.
terrain_preference: Technical, complex run-ins where pure sprinter trains lose cohesion. Thrives in transitional terrain requiring tactical awareness over pure power. Vulnerable on straightforward power sprints.
vulnerability: Outgunned in pure sprint scenarios, forcing desperate late-race gambits. Limited climbing ceiling despite domestique utility suggests can't follow sustained high-mountain pace.
key_patterns: Late-race attacks (sub-1km) on technical finishes when outmatched in pure sprints (2 instances)
Intermediate sprint bonus hunting as GC disruption tactic, not personal gain (2 instances)
High-mountain domestique work despite non-climber profile shows tactical versatility (2 Grand Tour appearances)
Targets transitional stages where sprinter team control weakens on complex parcours
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 Giro showed continued Grand Tour utility role, but no progression from opportunistic attacker to stage contention. Tactical niche remains consistent.
